title: 1. Client Overview
content:
- **Business name:** Augusta Custom Homes
- **Location / niche:** Custom home builder / contractor.
- **Website:** `https://www.augustach.com/`
- **Key contacts:** No direct Augusta stakeholder name was surfaced in the accessible project material.
- **GBP / social links:** Not surfaced in the reviewed material.
- **Services offered:** Custom homes / contractor work is implied by brand and niche. No service list was documented in the accessible material.
- **Target areas / locations:** Not confirmed in the reviewed material.

title: 2. Client Persona & Communication Style
content:
- **Unclear.** Augusta appears in the accessible project context mainly as an **audit / comparison subject**, not as a fully documented active client account.
- There is not enough evidence to characterize stakeholder communication style, update preferences, or relationship dynamics.

title: 3. Timeline of Key Events
content:
- **Before 2026-02-12:** Tanatsa asked for an audit of Augusta Custom Homes.
- In the internal framing of that audit, Augusta was described as being serviced by an **Indian team** that was cheaper than Donal’s service.
- Augusta was positioned as having **poor results** and **weak AI visibility**, especially relative to Lankford Roofing and Metal Warehouse.

title: 4. SEO / Delivery History
content:
- Based on the accessible project material, Augusta was **audited / reviewed**, but there is no reliable evidence here that Online Optimisers was actively delivering an SEO campaign for them.
- Observations captured in project context:
  - Results were described as poor
  - AI visibility was described as weak / “barely any”
  - The incumbent lower-cost team was framed negatively
- What is missing / unconfirmed:
  - Start date
  - Scope of work
  - Deliverables performed
  - Rankings, traffic, GBP metrics
  - Tools used on the account beyond the comparison reference

title: 5. Writing Style & Tone Used
content:
- Augusta was discussed in an audit / comparison context, not in customer-facing content production.
- Tone used internally was critical and comparative, mainly to highlight stronger results for Lankford and Metal Warehouse.
- No approved messaging, brand tone, or content style guidance for Augusta was surfaced.

title: 6. Ideas Generated (Used or Unused)
content:
- Use Augusta as a comparison point to strengthen the perceived value of stronger-performing accounts
- Use AI visibility / AI ranking as a differentiator versus cheaper SEO vendors
- No broader Augusta-specific strategy ideas were clearly documented in the material reviewed

title: 7. Results & Wins
content:
- No wins for Augusta were surfaced.
- The only substantive result-related note was negative: Augusta was framed as having **poor SEO / AI visibility performance** compared with Lankford and Metal Warehouse.

title: 8. Open Issues / Unfinished Business
content:
- Clarify whether Augusta is:
  - a prospect,
  - a one-off audit subject, or
  - an actual client with delivery history not visible in the current project context.
- If Augusta is active, a proper account reconstruction is needed from source systems before any work is resumed.

title: 9. Anything Else Worth Knowing
content:
- Treat this brief as **partial and low-confidence** because the accessible project material on Augusta is thin.
- The strongest usable takeaway is competitive positioning: Augusta was used as an example of weaker outcomes under a lower-cost provider.
- Do not assume scope, relationship status, or campaign history without validating against live records.
